rulings.Case Title: HEATHER GREGG VS. JAMES J. CUNNINGHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT JAMES CUNNINGHAM MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's fourth amended complaint is stricken. Plaintiff failed to comply with the Court's prior order to reconcile the inconsistent positions taken in 2019 filing and in this complaint regarding Plaintiff's claims to the real property and LLCs. Plaintiff failed to obtain leave of Court to allege a new cause of action (albeit references to constructive trust as a remedy existed in the prior version of the complaint as a remedy). Plaintiff further fails to cite any cases for the proposition that a constructive trust remedy is available under breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff is not precluded from seeking constructive trust as a remedy if causes of action in the amended complaint support such relief. Motion is granted as to any references to the real property asserted in conjunction with the claim to interest or value of the property. (This issue may be moot in light of Plaintiff's representation that she will not proceed with any of her property claims in this jurisdiction). Motion is granted as to paragraph 39 with leave to amend to explain the change from "5 years" to "7 years." Motion is denied at to paragraphs 11, 30 and 35. While these allegations are different from the prior version of the complaint, they are more expansive, and not entirely inconsistent to trigger the sham pleadings doctrine. Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions to support malice, oppression or fraud. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER GREGG VS. JAMES J. CUNNINGHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 26, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT JAMES CUNNINGHAM, JAMES CUNNINGHAM DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. As to the causes of action for breach of express and implied contract, the Court notes that these causes of action are no longer alleged in the alternative (as they have been alleged in the prior iteration of the complaint). This change in the allegations renders the complaint uncertain as to which contractual terms are express and which are implied. Allegations in the Second Amended Complaint list identical terms in paragraph 11 and paragraph 30. However there cannot be a valid, express contract and an implied contract, each embracing the same subject matter, existing at the same time. The only contractual term that is listed solely under the "implied contact" is contained in paragraph 35 regarding parties agreement to share their wealth and accumulation of assets during their relationship in equal shares. Plaintiff does not allege that she performed under this term or her performance was frustrated. Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege express and implied contracts in the alternative, to allege the formation and the terms of (1) the oral contract and (2) the formation and the terms of the implied contract. The Court further notes that in opposition to the Motion to Strike Plaintiff appears to suggest that she did not believe that the contracts (regarding sharing the assets accumulated during their relationship) were binding enforceable agreements until she consulted her attorneys sometime after defendant's purported breach in December 2021. This assertion (while not yet pled in any iteration of the complaint, but argued in opposition) suggests that at the time the contract(s) were formed (2013-2014) there could not have been mutual assent to the terms of the agreement. Therefore, Plaintiff shall allege facts regarding mutual assent to the formation of the contract(s). Demurrer to the cause of action for constructive trust is moot. See ruling on the Motion to Strike. Parties' requests for judicial notice are granted. Additionally, all declarations (except for meet and confer declaration) submitted in conjunction with the briefs on this action are stricken. There is no authority cited for the proposition that declarations may be considered on a demurrer. Finally, the last two pages of Plaintiff's Points and Authorities in support of the opposition are stricken. Plaintiff did not seek and did not receive a permission to file an oversized brief and therefore the brief is limited to 15 pages. In the future, if oversized briefs are submitted, the Court will strike the entire brief. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file an amended complaint. It appears that Plaintiff at this time wishes to remove any claims (ownership, value, etc) to the real property. If the amended complaint removes any claims associated with real property, all future motions shall be calendared in department 302. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
